top_gaps: Does not propose concrete mitigation strategies or quantify the rate of collapse across different model scales.
Does not evaluate mitigation strategies for preserving tail pathology in synthetic medical data generation pipelines.
Does not address language models or provide theoretical guarantees on when synthetic augmentation reliably prevents collapse.
Conditions for stability may be difficult to satisfy in practice on uncurated web-scale data.
Does not quantify real-world prevalence of synthetic training data contamination or provide mitigation strategies at scale.
Curation cost and feasibility at web scale are not addressed, leaving open how to obtain sufficient human feedback for practical stabilization.
Method is evaluated only on image generation; applicability to language models and real-world mixed human-synthetic corpora is unexamined.
Does not validate surplexity as a collapse detector on large-scale real-world web-contaminated corpora.
Focuses on computer vision continual learning and does not address analogous contamination effects in language model training pipelines.
Theory is primarily analytical and lacks large-scale empirical validation across diverse model architectures and training regimes.
Does not assess downstream effects of this contamination on models trained on scientific corpora, nor does it track quality degradation over time.
Does not assess whether AI-mixed content causes measurable quality degradation in downstream model training or correlate contamination with model collapse rates.
Does not track longitudinal trends beyond the crossover point or analyze topic/domain-level variation in AI vs. human content prevalence.
Does not quantify the rate of retrieval collapse or propose robust mitigation strategies beyond characterizing the failure mode.
Lacks large-scale ground-truth labeled datasets and robust detection methods tailored to the educational domain.
Empirical measurement of collapse thresholds and evaluation of countermeasures remain underexplored.
Does not quantify the actual prevalence of such AI-generated content in wildlife media or measure downstream effects on conservation behavior.
Methodological challenges in reliably distinguishing AI from human-authored text in non-English media remain unresolved.
Relies largely on theoretical and qualitative analysis without rigorous empirical measurement of bot-to-human content ratios.
Does not address how AI-generated political imagery affects downstream voting behavior or policy attitudes.
Detection performance may degrade as LLMs evolve, and the approach is limited to Turkish-language outlets without cross-lingual generalization.
Does not address downstream effects on training data pollution or the effectiveness of labeling at scale across diverse platforms.
Does not address downstream effects on training data pollution or the effectiveness of labeling at scale across diverse platforms.
Does not evaluate robustness to adversarial paraphrasing or examine detection limits as AI generators improve.
Does not address robustness to paraphrasing attacks or the scenario where watermarked text re-enters training data.
Retrieval-based defense depends on a clean reference corpus that may itself become polluted by AI-generated content.
Perplexity thresholds may shift as code-generation models improve, leaving the detector brittle over time.
Does not empirically evaluate detector robustness or address the feedback loop between detection and increasingly sophisticated generators.
Does not address how detector performance degrades as models are retrained on increasingly AI-contaminated corpora.
Does not address the broader challenge of detecting AI-generated content at scale or the arms race between generation and detection.
Does not fully analyze the performance-diversity trade-off or whether diversity loss is recoverable with data mixing strategies.
Authenticity is defined at the sample level and does not address downstream training effects of synthetic data on model capability or collapse.
Focused on a single language family and does not connect diversity metrics to training data quality or generative model behavior.
Does not connect diversity metrics to AI-generated text or recursive training dynamics.
Does not address synthetic or AI-generated text, nor implications for model training data quality.
Does not address corpus quality, synthetic data contamination, or recursive training degradation.
Focused on typological complexity comparisons and does not connect to AI-generated text or training data quality.
Does not connect lexical diversity metrics to AI-generated or synthetic text, limiting applicability to model collapse or corpus quality monitoring.
Does not examine how translationese effects compound under iterative or recursive training, leaving open questions about long-term corpus quality drift.
Does not address how evaluation reliability changes when test data is itself contaminated with AI-generated content or when distributions shift over time.
Does not isolate the contribution of CoT data versus base web-corpus quality to downstream reasoning gains.
Findings may not generalize beyond Romanian or other morphologically rich low-resource languages.
Does not provide detailed ablations isolating the impact of individual data curation decisions on benchmark performance.
Does not propose a definitive alternative filtering strategy that overcomes the identified limitations of classifier-based methods.
Does not assess long-term model collapse risks from recursive use of synthetically generated pretraining data.
Does not provide a robust alternative protocol that reliably bridges small-scale and full-scale data curation experiments.
Does not study temporal drift in extraction quality or how parser improvements interact with deduplication pipelines.
Does not evaluate how quality-diversity trade-offs evolve across training scale or in continual pretraining settings.
Does not address how sample-wise mixing scales to extremely large corpora or interacts with deduplication pipelines.
Does not provide longitudinal tracking of how web data quality evolves over time or address AI-generated content contamination.
Does not investigate whether authentic human behavioral data (rather than synthetic empathy data) mitigates these reliability trade-offs.
Does not evaluate whether self-generated alignment data degrades quality over iterative training cycles.

Does not address the quality or source of preference data used for ranking, leaving open questions about annotation cost reduction.

Focused on biomedical meta-analysis rather than AI training pipelines or web-scale text data provenance.

Domain-specific to archaeology and Indigenous heritage data; does not address AI training data pipelines or automated provenance tracking.
